macro_segments.title: Resh Lakish's Marking of Caves and the Heavenly Recognition of Rabbi Chiya's Deeds

micro_segments.title: Resh Lakish marking caves and his lament about not spreading Torah

Rabbi Chanina and Rabbi Chiya's dispute over Torah preservation through learning versus deeds

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Chiya's Sustainable System for Preserving Torah Through Teaching Children

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Chiya's method: planting flax, making nets, catching deer, and writing Torah scrolls
Teaching five children each book of the Chumash and six children the orders of Mishnah
The pedagogical power of making each child both learner and teacher
Rebbe's affirmation that the deeds of Chiya were great, and Rabbi Yishmael's comparison to his predecessors

macro_segments.title: Heavenly Visions: Rabbi Yosi bar Chanina's Spiritual Status and the Celestial Academy

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Zeira's dream vision of the hierarchy of sages in the heavenly academy
Rabbi Yosi bar Chanina appearing to Rabbi Zeira and explaining why Rabbi Yochanan cannot approach Rabbi Chiya's chariot
The story of Rabbi Chaviva bar Surmti witnessing the fire of Rabbi Chiya's chariot ascending to heaven
Rabbi Chaviva's healing after prostrating at Rabbi Chiya's cave and pledging devotion to his teachings

macro_segments.title: Eliyahu's Service to the Patriarchs and the Power of Rabbi Chiya's Prayer

micro_segments.title: Eliyahu visits Rebbe's academy and explains his delayed arrival due to waking the Patriarchs
The heavenly concern that Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob's prayers might bring Mashiach prematurely
Rebbe's attempt to bring Mashiach by organizing a communal fast with Rabbi Chiya and his sons as prayer leaders
Eliyahu's punishment and intervention to stop the prayer before Mechaye Meisim is uttered

macro_segments.title: Samuel the Physician and the Limits of Ordination

micro_segments.title: Samuel as Rebbe's physician and Rebbe's failed attempts to ordain him
Samuel's revelation from the book of Adam Harashon that he will be called wise but not rabbi
Connection between Samuel's title of 'chacham' and the ruling that we follow his opinions in monetary law

macro_segments.title: The Editing of Mishnah by Rebbe and the Compilation of Gemara by Rav Ashi and Ravina

micro_segments.title: Samuel's vision of the heavenly book stating that Rebbe and Rabbi Natan ended the Tannaitic period
Rashi's explanation of Rebbe's role in organizing and systematizing all prior Tannaitic material into the Mishnah
Rav Ashi and Ravina as the end of the Amoraic period and their role in organizing and finalizing the Gemara
Scholarly debate on whether Rav Ashi and Ravina authored the anonymous voice of the Gemara

macro_segments.title: Rabbah bar Nachmani's Hiding from the Government and His Heavenly Debate

micro_segments.title: Government slander against the Jewish people regarding Rabbah bar Nachmani's educational disruption of tax collection
The king's officer sent to capture Rabbah, his sickness at the inn, and Rabbah's healing advice
Rabbah's escape and continued hiding as the officer pursues him across multiple locations
The heavenly debate over ritual purity of tzaraat with spots and white hairs
Rabbah bar Nachmani summoned to heaven as the deciding voice in the heavenly court
Rabbah's death and declaration of 'tahor' as his soul departs, siding with God's position
The heavenly announcement of Rabbah's calling and the rabbis' inability to locate or properly bury his body
The Arab carrying Rabbah's body being lifted by heavenly wind and thrown across the river
The Arab's prayer to God calming the heavenly anger over Rabbah's improper burial

micro_segments.title: The story of Rabbi Yochanan ben Matya's promise of food and his father's teaching about Bnei Avraham Yitzchak v'Yaakov

macro_segments.title: Comparative Feasts: Solomon's Grandeur versus Abraham's Hospitality

micro_segments.title: Solomon's daily provisions: flour, cattle, sheep, and wild animals—the extent of his royal feasting
The interpretation that Solomon's bread was merely for cleaning pot overflow and his meat was appetizers
Solomon's thousand wives each preparing identical feasts, each hoping he would dine at her house
Abraham's more modest but spiritually greater feast when he received the three angels
The derivation that Abraham provided three calves, each with butter, milk, and a special tongue preparation
The conclusion that breaking a covenant promise to Bnei Avraham Yitzchak v'Yaakov creates an endless obligation
